title: trucateContext
description: a User command to truncate the context to specific lentgh

title: Secret
description:
Screen every tool output for secrets, using a library to detect secrets.
  - secrets are API keys, passwords and other similar information
  - use "detect-secrets" installable by "pip install detect-secrets"
    - basic usage is something like:
      ```
      from detect_secrets import SecretsCollection
      from detect_secrets.settings import default_settings
      
      secrets = SecretsCollection()
      with default_settings():
          secrets.scan_file('test_data/config.ini')
      
      
      import json
      print(json.dumps(secrets.json(), indent=2))
      ```
  - edit secrets out by default, using placeholders
  - there should be a flag to show secrets in tool outputs, toggled by a command, set to mask secrets out by default
  - while we are handling tool output and filtering content, we should also filter for strange characters that could mess with our terminal display settings

description: Create tests for sandboxing in eggthreads

title: DropMessage
description: A command to drop context part which is from last User message (including the last User message) till the end of context

title: egg-w3e follow-up: token accounting
description:
Let's get a more realistic cost estimate / exact calculation when the information is available from the api.
 - ```
 ... import os, httpx
>>> def send_chat_request(conversation_messages):
...     # Set your API key in OPENAI_API_KEY env variable (never hard-code it).
...     api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
...     if not api_key:
...         raise ValueError("Set OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable!")
...     api_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions"
...     headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"}
...     payload = {"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "messages": conversation_messages,
...                "max_completion_tokens": 2345}
...     try:
...         response = httpx.post(api_url, headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=180.0)
...         response.raise_for_status()
...     except Exception as error:
...         print("API error:", error)
...         return None
...     return response
... 
>>> system = [{"role": "system", "content":
...            "You are a helpful AI assistant."}]
>>> user = [{"role": "user", "content":
...            "Are OpenAI AI models expensive on API?"}]
>>> response = send_chat_request(system+user)
```
  - >> response.json()["usage"]
   {
     'prompt_tokens': 27,
     'completion_tokens': 128,
     'total_tokens': 155,
     'prompt_tokens_details': {
       'cached_tokens': 0,
       'audio_tokens': 0
       },
     'completion_tokens_details': {
       'reasoning_tokens': 0,
       'audio_tokens': 0,
       'accepted_prediction_tokens': 0,
       'rejected_prediction_tokens': 0
       }
    }

If we get this from the api, we could return them from eggllm, and perhaps emmit usage events in eggthreads to calculate preciselly the cost for the Thread.
  - Usage events would contain
    - model
      - with current model pricing
    - the usage object
  - eggthreads would accumulate those events to calculate real api cost

title: Sandboxing
description:
This should be a feature of eggthreads library.
Implement tool execution sandboxing using
  - @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime
    - installed with npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sanbox-runtime
    - If not installed and enabled, there should be a red notice that is displayed the whole time in "System" panel that we are running without a sandbox
    - used as a command line tool:
      ```bash
      # Run a command in the sandbox
      srt echo "hello world"
      
      # With debug logging
      srt --debug curl https://example.com
      
      # Specify custom settings file
      srt --settings /path/to/srt-settings.json npm install
      ```
    - configuration example
      ```json
      {
        "network": {
          "allowedDomains": [
            "github.com",
            "*.github.com",
            "lfs.github.com",
            "api.github.com",
            "npmjs.org",
            "*.npmjs.org"
          ],
          "deniedDomains": ["malicious.com"],
          "allowUnixSockets": ["/var/run/docker.sock"],
          "allowLocalBinding": false
        },
        "filesystem": {
          "denyRead": ["~/.ssh"],
          "allowWrite": [".", "src/", "test/", "/tmp"],
          "denyWrite": [".env", "config/production.json"]
        },
        "ignoreViolations": {
          "*": ["/usr/bin", "/System"],
          "git push": ["/usr/bin/nc"],
          "npm": ["/private/tmp"]
        },
        "enableWeakerNestedSandbox": false
      }
      ```
    - Configuration management:
      - There should be a default configuration for srt
        - On the filesystem only the current directory can be written to or read from
        - For sandboxing there should be a file ./.egg/srt/default.json, which is used as a default config for new Threads whose working directory is ./ . 
          If it doesn't exist, the file is created using reasonable defaults (only read/write from current directory and subdirectories, without being able to write into ./.egg/srt/default.json file.
      - There should be a command that sets the srt configuration from now on, for example something like
        - /setThreadSandboxConfig readall.json
          - this command is for the egg frontend
      - eggthreads should export api function to set srt configuration for the Thread
      - Any srt config that is set as the active config, should not be able to override the rule of not being able to write into default.json config.                                        
      - Per thread configs should be written as events in the db, which contain the full config json.                                                                                        
      - There should only be commands to set the current Thread config, which should be honored by newly created children, and by old children which have no config set explicitly.          
      - All the commands should have autocomplete, and suggestions working.                                                                                                                  
      - There is no such thing as process-wide sandbox configuration, only the default configuration, which is used if there is no configuration set for the Thread, or any of the ancestors.
Please check if this is so, and correct if not.

title: tool_calls_must_be_followed_by_tool_messages_bug
description:
Sometimes when a tool call is interrupted (not sure if that is the only case when, might also be when some approval is not given (e.g. output approval?)) we get:
  ```
  Runner error: HTTP 400: {
    "error": {
      "message": "An assistant message with 'tool_calls' must be followed by tool messages responding to each 'tool_call_id'. The following tool_call_ids did not have response messages: call_g8izM4JBdrx4WBr8CqrhdIYm",
      "type": "invalid_request_error",
      "param": "messages.[107].role",
      "code": null
    }
  }

  ```

This happens for example if one enters some different than expected text while the TUI is waiting for output approval response (then that text gets sent as a user message, before the tool reponse).
Think realy hard how can we avoid the scenario where this messes the Threads ability to continue, because the error persists. We should not use a hack to solve this, but a genuine solution is needed.
Maybe we can prevent adding User messages while tool calls are unanswered in a more general way also?

description:

    - The obvious thing happens on '$$ "ls -la"'

title: tokenCount
description:
We should use the current snapshot to calculate approximatelly:
  - context length
  - per message, reasoning, tool call, tool response, user command, user command response ... token counts
    - output them in the title of the appropriate boxes that contain them in static view
  - approximate api token usage
    - presumably cached tokens
    - total sent (input) tokens
    - total output tokens by the llms over the api
    - these things should be displayed in "Chat Messages" title
    - to calculate these, we do out best to guess api turns from the snapshot
  - this should be implemented in eggthreads
    - using tiktoken, cl100k_base tokenizer for ALL llm's (we don't mind being a little off)

title: AI.md
description: Use AI.md in the working folder as an addition to system prompt

title: Testing
description:
Create tests for all the functionality of the code, so that when we develop something we know we didn't break something.
  - you need to create tests for this folder (./) which contains the "egg" project, for ../eggthreads, ../eggdisplay, and ../eggllm libraries.
    - the tests should be in <libraryFolder>/tests for each of the 4 git repositories (egg, eggthreads, eggdisplay, eggllm)
  - first carefuly create a plan for testing, because we want to come as close as possible to end-to-end / functional testing
  - then go ahead and start writing tests
